volumeTitle: Amphitryon. The Comedy of Asses. The Pot of Gold. The Two Bacchises. The Captives
label: The Comedy of Asses (Section 3.3.626 - Section 3.3.690)

sections.label: Section 3.3.626
sections.original: Tum tu igitur loquere quod lubet .
sections.translation: For that reason , then , say on what you please .

sections.label: Section 3.3.626b
sections.original: Hunc hercle verberare .
sections.translation: Troth , I ’ d like to give him a beating .

sections.label: Section 3.3.627
sections.original: Quisnam istuc adcredat tibi , cinaede calamistrate ?
sections.translation: Who , pray , would allow you to do so , you frizzle- pated [*] mountebank ? Could you thrash me , you , who reckon as your daily food your own thrashings ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.629
sections.original: Vt vostrae fortunae meis praecedunt , Libane , longe ,
sections.translation: How far superior , Libanus , are your lots to my own ,

sections.label: Section 3.3.630
sections.original: qui hódie numquam ad vesperum vivam .
sections.translation: who never will live this day until the evening .

sections.label: Section 3.3.630b
sections.original: Quapropter , quaeso ?
sections.translation: For what reason , prithee ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.631
sections.original: Quia ego hanc amó et haec me amat , huic quod dem nusquam quicquam est ,
sections.translation: Because I ’ m in love with her , and she ’ s in love with me , and nowhere have I anything to bestow upon her ; for that reason has her mother expelled me with all my affection from her house . The twenty minae of silver have brought me to my end , which the young man , Diabolus , declared that he would give her this day ,

sections.label: Section 3.3.635
sections.original: ut hanc ne quoquam mitteret nisi ad se hunc annum totum .
sections.translation: in order that she mightn ’ t send her anywhere , for this whole year , except to himself . Don ’ t you see of what force are twenty minae of silver , or what they can effect ? The man who parts with them is happy ; I , who part not with them , am undone .

sections.label: Section 3.3.638
sections.original: Iam dedit argentum ?
sections.translation: Has he already paid [*] the money ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.638b
sections.original: Non dedit .
sections.translation: He hasn ’ t paid it .

sections.label: Section 3.3.638c
sections.original: Bono animo es , ne formida .
sections.translation: Be of good courage ; don ’ t be afraid .

sections.label: Section 3.3.639
sections.original: Secede huc , Libane , te volo .
sections.translation: Step this way , Libanus , I want you .

sections.label: Section 3.3.639b
sections.original: Si quid vis .
sections.translation: Certainly , if you want anything .

sections.label: Section 3.3.639c
sections.original: Obsecro vos ,
sections.translation: I entreat of you ,

sections.label: Section 3.3.640
sections.original: eadem istac opera suaviust complexos fabulari .
sections.translation: is it more pleasant in this same matter for you to discourse hugging one another ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.641
sections.original: Non omnia eadem aeque omnibus , ere , suavia esse scito :
sections.translation: Understand , master , that all things are not equally sweet to all persons . ’ Tis pleasant for you lovers to converse , hugging one another ; I care nothing for his hugging ( pointing to LEONIDA ) , and ( pointing to PHILENIUM ) she despises mine . Do you then yourself do that which you would be suggesting to us to do .

sections.label: Section 3.3.645
sections.original: Ego vero , et quidem edepol lubens . interea , si videtur ,
sections.translation: Indeed I will , and really with pleasure , i ’ faith . ( Placing his arm round PHILENIUM ’ S neck .) In the meantime , if it seems good to you , do you step aside there .

sections.label: Section 3.3.646b
sections.original: Vin erum deludi ?
sections.translation: Should you like our master to be bantered a bit ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.646c
sections.original: Dignust sane .
sections.translation: He really is deserving of it .

sections.label: Section 3.3.647
sections.original: Vin faciam ut te Philaenium praesente hoc amplexetur ?
sections.translation: Should you like me , in his presence , to make Philenium embrace me ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.648
sections.original: Cupio hercle .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I should like it .

sections.label: Section 3.3.648b
sections.original: Sequere hac .
sections.translation: Follow me this way .

sections.label: Section 3.3.648c
sections.original: Ecquid est salutis ? satis locuti .
sections.translation: Is there any escape at all ? Have you conversed enough ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.649
sections.original: Auscultate atque operam date et mea dicta devorate .
sections.translation: Listen , and give attention , and devour my words .

sections.label: Section 3.3.650
sections.original: primum omnium servos tuos nos esse non negamus ;
sections.translation: First of all , that we are your slaves , we don ’ t deny ; but if twenty silver minae are forthcoming for you , by what name will you call us ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.652b
sections.original: Libertos .
sections.translation: Freed-men .

sections.label: Section 3.3.652c
sections.original: Non patronos ?
sections.translation: And not patrons ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.653
sections.original: Id potius .
sections.translation: That in preference .

sections.label: Section 3.3.653b
sections.original: Viginti minae hic insunt in crumina ,
sections.translation: Here are twenty minae in this bag . These , if you like , I ’ ll give you .

sections.label: Section 3.3.654b
sections.original: Di te servassint semper ,
sections.translation: May the Deities ever preserve you ,

sections.label: Section 3.3.655
sections.original: custos erilis , decus popli , thensaurus copiarum ,
sections.translation: protector of your master , honor to the people , treasury of resources , preserver of my inner man [*] , and commander of love ; place it here , put down that bag , here on the spot , at once [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.3.658
sections.original: Nolo ego te , quí erus sis , mihi onus istuc sustinere .
sections.translation: I don ’ t like you , who are my master , to carry this load .

sections.label: Section 3.3.659
sections.original: Quin tu labore liberas te atque istam imponis in me ?
sections.translation: Still , do you rid yourself of the trouble , and fasten that bag to myself .

sections.label: Section 3.3.660
sections.original: Ego baiulabo , tu , ut decet dominum , ante me ito inanis .
sections.translation: I ’ ll carry it , porter-like ; you , as befits my master , go , without any burden , before me .

sections.label: Section 3.3.661
sections.original: Quid nunc ?
sections.translation: How now ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.661b
sections.original: Quid est ?
sections.translation: Why ’ s this ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.661c
sections.original: Quin tradis huc cruminam pressatum umerum ?
sections.translation: Why don ’ t you give up the bag [*] here , for your master to feel its weight ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.662
sections.original: Hanc , cui , iube petere atque orare mecum .
sections.translation: Bid her to whom I ’ m about to give it , to beg and entreat it of me . For that ’ s a dangerous spot where you bid me put it down at once .

sections.label: Section 3.3.664
sections.original: Da , meus ocellus , mea rosa , mi ánime , mea voluptas ,
sections.translation: Apple of my eye , my rose , my life , my delight ,

sections.label: Section 3.3.665
sections.original: Leonida , argentum mihi , ne nos diiunge amantis .
sections.translation: Leonida , do give me the money , and don ’ t sever us lovers asunder .

sections.label: Section 3.3.666
sections.original: Dic me igitur tuom passerculum , gallinam , coturnicem ,
sections.translation: Call me , then , your little sparrow , your chicken , your quail , your pet lamb : say that I ’ m your pet kid or your pet calf ; take me by the ears , press your lips to my lips .

sections.label: Section 3.3.669
sections.original: Ten osculetur , verbero ?
sections.translation: She , kiss you , you whip-scoundrel ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.669b
sections.original: Quam vero indignum visum est ?
sections.translation: Really , how unbecoming it does seem !

sections.label: Section 3.3.670
sections.original: at qui pol hodie non feres , ni genua confricantur .
sections.translation: But , by the powers , you shan ’ t get it this day , if my knees are not embraced .

sections.label: Section 3.3.671
sections.original: Quidvis egestas imperat : fricentur . dan quod oro ?
sections.translation: Necessity compels to anything . ( To LEONIDA .) Let them be embraced ( kneels down and embraces his knees ) : now give what I ’ m asking for .

sections.label: Section 3.3.672
sections.original: Age , mi Leonida , obsecro , fer amanti ero salutem ,
sections.translation: Come , my Leonida , prithee do bring safety to your master thus in love . Redeem yourself from him by this service , and purchase him for yourself with this money .

sections.label: Section 3.3.674
sections.original: Nimis bélla es atque amabilis , et si hoc meum esset , hodie
sections.translation: You are very pretty and amiable ; and if this were mine , you should never this day

sections.label: Section 3.3.675
sections.original: numquam me orares quin darem : illum te orare meliust ,
sections.translation: ask me for it , but I would give it you : ’ tis better for you to ask it of him ( pointing to LIBANUS ) , for ’ twas he gave it me to keep for him . Approach him then prettily , my pretty one . ( Delivers the bag to LIBANUS .) Take this , please , Libanus .

sections.label: Section 3.3.677b
sections.original: Furcifer , etiam me delusisti ?
sections.translation: Scoundrel , are you still trifling with me ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.678
sections.original: Numquam hercle facerem , genua ni tam nequiter fricares .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I should never have done so , if you hadn ’ t embraced my knees so roughly . ( Aside to LIBANUS .) Come , please , in your turn , do you at once have some sport with him , and give her an embrace .

sections.label: Section 3.3.680
sections.original: Taceas , me spectes .
sections.translation: Hold your tongue ; trust me for that .

sections.label: Section 3.3.680b
sections.original: Quin ad hunc , Philaenium , adgredimur ,
sections.translation: Why don ’ t we accost him , Philenium ? ( pointing to LIBANUS ) — really , a very worthy fellow , upon my faith , and not like this thief ( pointing to LEONIDA ) .

sections.label: Section 3.3.682
sections.original: Inambulandum est : nunc mihi vicissim supplicabunt .
sections.translation: We must walk up and down ; now , in my turn , they ’ ll be entreating me .

sections.label: Section 3.3.683
sections.original: Quaeso hercle , Libane , sis erum tuis factis sospitari ,
sections.translation: By heaven , Libanus , I do entreat you , be pleased by your deeds to come to your master ’ s rescue ; do give me those twenty minae : you see that thus in love I stand in need of them .

sections.label: Section 3.3.685
sections.original: Videbitur . factum volo . redito huc conticinno .
sections.translation: It shall be seen to ; I wish it done ; return here at nightfall . Now bid her , ever so little , to beg and entreat them of me .

sections.label: Section 3.3.687
sections.original: Amandone exorarier vis ted an osculando ?
sections.translation: Do you wish me to begin with caressing , or with kissing you ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.688
sections.original: Enim véro utrumque .
sections.translation: Why , really , with them both .

sections.label: Section 3.3.688b
sections.original: Ergo , obsecro , ét tu utrumque nostrum serva .
sections.translation: And do you then , I do entreat you , prove the saving of us both .

sections.label: Section 3.3.689
sections.original: O Libane , mi patrone , mi trade istuc . magis decorumst
sections.translation: O Libanus , my patron , do give me that ; ’ tis more becoming

sections.label: Section 3.3.690
sections.original: libertum potius quam patronum onus in via portare .
sections.translation: for the freed-man [*] , than for the patron , to be carrying a burden in the street .
